title: Custom dashboard vs dashboard layout vs saved view vs preference center
summary: Use custom dashboard for persisted widget composition, dashboard layout for a coherent fixed monitoring page, saved view for one data surface's presentation state, and preference center for account or communication preferences.
decisionRules: Choose custom dashboard when users select widgets, arrange order, resize tiles, save personal or shared layouts, and need reset or copy paths across sessions.
Choose dashboard layout when the main problem is arranging a product-defined monitoring page with hierarchy, filters, freshness, and drill paths rather than user-authored composition.
Choose saved view when users persist columns, filters, sort, grouping, layout mode, or density for one table, board, list, or record surface.
Choose preference center when users configure communication, privacy, cookie, language, notification, or account preference categories rather than dashboard widgets.
A custom dashboard must separate layout edits from data filters and from saved views embedded inside widgets, because each has different persistence and audience impact.
Use personal dashboard save when the composition affects one user; require shared-edit permission, audience warning, and rollback when changes affect a team dashboard.
Use dashboard layout with optional collapse or filter controls when users do not need to add, remove, reorder, resize, or replace widgets.
Use saved view for reusable presentation of one changing dataset; use custom dashboard when several widgets, reports, saved views, shortcuts, or sources are composed together.
Do not hide required operational, policy, or compliance widgets through customization unless the UI explains the policy lock or governed exception path.
When responsive order differs from desktop order, preview the custom dashboard's mobile stack before saving so critical widgets are not buried.
failureModes: A fixed dashboard with filters is labelled custom even though users cannot choose widgets, order, or size.
A drag-only dashboard rearranges cards visually while keyboard order and mobile order stay unchanged.
Saving a personal dashboard silently overwrites the shared team dashboard.
A saved view inside a dashboard widget is edited and users think they changed the dashboard layout.
A preference center is used for dashboard widgets, mixing consent and account settings with analytics layout controls.
Permission-limited or retired widgets become empty spaces with no replace, remove, or request-access path.

sources.note: Microsoft documents dashboard tiles as pinned dashboard composition units.
Microsoft documents dashboard hierarchy, context, clean layout, and prominent important information.
Tableau documents dashboard layout containers, sizing, tiled and floating layout, grouping, and responsive behavior.
HubSpot documents saved view visibility and presentation persistence that differ from multi-widget dashboard composition.
